programmes.show_description: The Blue Carbuncle. Enjoy this classic short story read by Mike Read where Sherlock Holmes investigates a rare gem known as the Blue Carbuncle.
Best Mates. Stars: Adam Faith, Iain Cuthbertson & Lynn Dalby. Budgie comes to the aid of his best friend Maguire, but this simply leads them both into further bother.
1993. Drama. Director: Emir Kusturica. Stars Johnny Depp, Faye Dunaway, Vincent Gallo. A young New Yorker goes to Arizona for a wedding and becomes intertwined in the lives of two eccentric women.
1949. A mysterious baby is found in a boat at sea, along with a charming portrait of a community in Coverack. Where is he from? Stars Dorothea Paul Directed by Anthony Mavrogordato.

programmes.show_name: Look At Life There Ought To Be a Law
programmes.show_description: 1968. With Sam Kydd. A light-hearted look at some of the laws you never even thought existed, and their pitfalls.
The Squatter. 1956. Stars Scott Forbes, Jeanne Moody & Steven Geray. Jim Bowie gives an arrogant Southern belle a lesson by persuading her to allow an impoverished family to remain on her land.
1959. Crime. Director: Ernest Morris. Stars Gordon Jackson, Sarah Lawson, Warren Mitchell. A shopkeeper is taken hostage when 3 crooks break into his shop to drill through to the bank next door.
1955. Directed by John Gilling and starring Alex Nicol. Two brothers are embroiled in a racket to smuggle a valuable painting. When a murder is committed they must try and clear their names.

programmes.show_name: Look At Life All in a Day's Work
programmes.show_description: 1969. A look at people who travel the world by air as part of their everyday jobs, including businessmen and women who fly around the world as easily as taking a taxi.

programmes.show_name: Four Star Playhouse A Kiss for Mr Lincoln
programmes.show_description: 1955. Stars Joanne Dru & Dick Foran. Henry tries to persuade Murdock to support Lincoln for president, but Murdock is only interested in Henry's wife...
1948. Horror. Directed by Oswald Mitchell and screenplay by John Gilling. Set in Edinburgh in the 1800s and starring Tod Slaughter as William Hart the infamous body broker.
1958. Thriller. Director: Ronald Kinnoch. Stars Marshall Thompson, John Loder & Henry Oscar. A physicist finds himself drawn into an investigation to track down a spy at his research station.
1964. Thriller. Director: Bryan Forbes. Stars Richard Attenborough, Nanette Newman & Kim Stanley. A medium stages a kidnapping which she will then 'solve' by pretending to be psychic.
1954. Comedy. Director: Val Guest. Stars Frankie Howerd, Margaret Rutherford & Petula Clark. An inept bus driver gets lost in fog with a group of passengers, on a coach laden with stolen gold.
1948. Drama. Directed by Arthur Crabtree. Stars Greta Gynt, John McCallum & Raymond Lovell. A racehorse owner becomes involved in a scandal after losing his money and his fiancée.
The Other Side of Swan. 1961. Stars Peter Graves, Anthony Wickert & Nigel Lovell. The governor asks Cobb to go to Melbourne and find his estranged brother who is believed to be dead.
The Finny Foot Affair. 1964. Stars Robert Vaughn, David McCallum, Kurt Russell & Leo G. Carroll. Solo is trying to track down a chemical agent that hastens the aging process.
Morna. Stars John Gregson, Alexander Davion and Daphne Anderson. After a beautiful blonde is found murdered her story is unravelled in flashbacks about her relationship to her posh school friends.
2003. Comedy/Drama. Director: Geoffrey Sax. Stars Penelope Keith, June Brown & Alan David. A wealthy housewife and her maid go on the run after attacking a house burglar and leaving him for dead.
Caroline Munro is back in The Cellar Club and ready to bring you another evening full of mystery and suspense with the twisted tale Tennis Court, directed by Cyril Frankel.

programmes.show_name: Hammer House of Mystery and Suspense
programmes.show_description: Tennis Court. 1984. Stars Peter Graves & Hannah Gordan. A couple move into a house they have inherited, but it soon becomes apparent that the old tennis court is far from normal.
Join Caroline Munro as she gives a glimpse at the Hammer treats coming up next week.
Everybody loves a baby. Stars: Adam Faith, Iain Cuthbertson & Lynn Dalby. Budgie starts a farming business, but not one that employs a farm or animals.

programmes.show_name: Eastbourne: Sun Trap of the South
programmes.show_description: Charming 60s travelogue inviting us to enjoy the delights of Eastbourne, where the sun always shines!
1949. Drama. Classic tale of a war romance featuring the smash hit music of The Legend of The Glass Mountain. Stars Michael Denison, Dulcie Gray & Valentina Cortese, with vocals from Tito Gobbi.
Mr Meglomania. 1961. Stars Stephen McNally, Robert Harland & Harold J. Stone. Investigative reporters Paul Marino & Jack Flood investigate various cases of industrial corruption. (S1, E08)
Sticks and Stones. Stars John Ericson, Peggy McCay & Mort Mills. British actor David Niven, introduces this 1950s US anthology series. (S1, E10)
The Critter Doctor. 1964. Stars Irene Ryan, Mark Goddard & Russell Collins. Granny gets frustrated when she confuses a house-to-house insecticide salesman with a critter doctor.

programmes.show_name: Look At Life The Other Film World
programmes.show_description: 1965. A marvelous look at how the film camera is used in science and industry to increase an individual's knowledge.
1954. Comedy. Director: Maurice Elvey. Stars Petula Clark, Donald Houston & Brenda de Banzie. Story of a Welsh postman's efforts to bring happiness into the lives of an assortment of people.
1953. Comedy. Penniless couple Roger (Bernard Braden) and Jean (Barbara Kelly) will get £10,000 if Roger can live a sober and profitable life. Starring Reg Dixon and John Laurie.
1961. An intriguing look at the world of lifts, with a study of the problems faced by engineers to get office workers up to the top of tall buildings.
1960. Drama. Stars Norman Wooland, Jane Hylton & Dennis Waterman. An ex-convict kidnaps his son and takes him on a train to Inverness, unaware he is putting his son's life in danger.
1958. Horror. Directed by Henry Cass and starring Sir Donald Wolfit. In the late 19th Century, the anaemic Dr Callistratus is the governor of a brutal penal institution in a remote castle.
1946. Thriller. Stars Oskar Homolka, Derek Farr & Muriel Pavlow. Antique dealer Mr. Heiss must deal with a blackmailer who knows of his criminal past.
The Golden Road. 1976. Stars Richard Easton & Robin Chadwick. Brian tries to change Jane's position on the golden road to Istanbul. The board members cast their votes on the Middle East project.
Death Ride. 1958. Stars Robert Beatty, Patrick Barr & Humphrey Heathcote. A man is suspected of murdering his wife; Maguire starts investigating, which motivates someone to try to run him over.
1978. Drama. Directed by Don Sharp. Stars Beau Bridges, Robert Powell & Jane Seymour. Lieutenant Faversham dishonours his regiment and loses his fiancée, but can he regain his honour and his love?

programmes.show_name: A Lifetime Ago with The Baim Collection
programmes.show_description: The Mood Man. 1965. A short film of Britain's greatest bandleader Joe Loss and his orchestra.
1993. Documentary series that acts as a tribute to the greats of British cinema. This episode focuses on a brilliant tribute to Anna Neagle.
21st Anniversary Programme. 1981. Skate fishing, traction engines, gun dogs and a dancing horse all feature in this special compilation celebrating '21 years of gossiping about the countryside'.
1957. The Mail Van Murder. Directed by John Knight. Starring Hy Hazell, Dennis Castle and Gordon Needham. Supt. Hammond hunts down the boss of a gang that stole a mail van. (S1, E23)
Albatross. 1980. Stars Bryan Marshall, Clifford Rose & Pamela Salem. Burton reshapes the company and ''special cargo'' looms. Personal complications arrive for Tony.
1981. Horror. Director: Lindsey C. Vickers. Stars Edward Woodward, Jane Merrow & Samantha Weysom. Ian misses his daughter's concert so he will have to contend with more than just childish sulking.
1962. Sci-Fi. A spectacular meteor storm blinds the world, except Bill. He, and a few others, must battle with flesh eating plants. Stars Nicole Maurey, Howard Keel, Janette Scott & Kieron Moore.
